commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c632f9b2b3de5d132374a79e8f502aae58e2d41d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an execution-key lookup so matching scoped running background exec calls can return an existing process session instead of spawning another child process.

PR surface: Source +141, Tests +266. Total +407 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main auto-backgrounds omitted-parameter exec calls through the default yield window and registers a fresh process session for each invocation; I did not run a live provider replay.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the patch misses the default auto-yield path and the external contributor must supply matching real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(exec): reuse duplicate background sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Repeated identical scoped background/yield `exec` retries now reuse the existing running process session instead of launching another child process.
- The reuse key is conservative: command, resolved cwd, scoped process identity, and resolved execution options must match.
- Plain foreground reruns, completed reruns, different env/PTY/timeout/cwd/scope/session identities, and normal `process` polling behavior remain separate.

## AI assistance

This PR was implemented with Codex assistance. I reviewed the change and understand the affected `exec` / `process` runtime path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
Repeated identical scoped background `exec` calls after a running result reuse the existing background session instead of launching duplicate processes.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix-62432`, using the real `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` implementations with isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`. No live provider, channel, or credential state was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
mkdir -p .artifacts/pr-proof
proof_log=".artifacts/pr-proof/62432-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ).log"
export HOME="$(mktemp -d)"
export OPENCLAW_HOME="$HOME/.openclaw"
export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$HOME/.openclaw-state"
export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$HOME/openclaw-config.json"
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF' 2>&1 | tee "$proof_log"
import { createExecTool, createProcessTool } from "./src/agents/bash-tools.ts";
import { resetProcessRegistryForTests } from "./src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts";

resetProcessRegistryForTests();

const scopeKey = "proof:issue-62432";
const command = "sleep 30";
const execTool = createExecTool({
  host: "gateway",
  security: "full",
  ask: "off",
  allowBackground: true,
  backgroundMs: 0,
  notifyOnExit: false,
  scopeKey,
  sessionKey: "agent:proof:issue-62432",
  cwd: process.cwd(),
});
const processTool = createProcessTool({ scopeKey });
let callId = 0;
const exec = async () => execTool.execute(`proof-exec-${++callId}`, { command, background: true });
const runProcess = async (args) => processTool.execute(`proof-process-${++callId}`, args);

const first = await exec();
const second = await exec();
const listed = await runProcess({ action: "list" });
const runningSessions = (listed.details.sessions ?? []).filter((session) => session.status === "running");

console.log(JSON.stringify({
  firstStatus: first.details.status,
  secondStatus: second.details.status,
  firstSessionId: first.details.sessionId,
  secondSessionId: second.details.sessionId,
  reusedSession: first.details.sessionId === second.details.sessionId,
  runningSessionCount: runningSessions.length,
  runningSessionIds: runningSessions.map((session) => session.sessionId),
  duplicateExecFanoutPrevented:
    first.details.sessionId === second.details.sessionId && runningSessions.length === 1,
}, null, 2));

await runProcess({ action: "kill", sessionId: first.details.sessionId });
resetProcessRegistryForTests();
EOF
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "firstStatus": "running",
  "secondStatus": "running",
  "firstSessionId": "lucky-mist",
  "secondSessionId": "lucky-mist",
  "reusedSession": true,
  "runningSessionCount": 1,
  "runningSessionIds": [
    "lucky-mist"
  ],
  "duplicateExecFanoutPrevented": true
}
```

```txt
proof_log=.artifacts/pr-proof/62432-20260601T135843Z.log
proof_status=0
```

Observed result after fix:
Both background `exec` calls returned `status: "running"` with the same session id, and `process list` reported one running session.

What was not tested:
No live Xiaomi/MiMo replay or provider-driven agent turn was run. The proof exercises the runtime/tool path directly; GitHub CI covers the broader platform matrix.

## Root Cause

`handleExec` called `runExecProcess` before returning the running-session guidance. `runExecProcess` generated and registered a fresh process session for each call. The registry had id/list lookups, but no scoped same-execution lookup for an already-backgrounded in-flight process.

## Dependency contract

External dependencies: none.

Source and docs checked:

- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts`
- `docs/gateway/background-process.md`
- `docs/tools/exec.md`

Contract used here: backgrounded sessions are managed through the `process` tool, are scoped by process identity, and are only pollable/listed when backgrounded. This patch stays inside that existing registry contract and does not change public tool schemas.

## Regression Test Plan

- Added registry coverage for exact command/cwd/execution-key scoped matches, missing execution identity, mismatched scope/session, different command, different cwd, and foreground sessions.
- Added behavior coverage that repeated identical scoped background `exec` calls return the same session id and only one running session is visible.
- Added coverage for overlapping explicit background retries while the first spawn is still in flight.
- Added negative coverage that env, PTY, and timeout drift start distinct sessions.
- Added negative coverage that a plain foreground rerun is not collapsed into an active background session.
- Added runtime cleanup coverage for pre-spawn setup failures after an explicit background session is registered.

## Security Impact

No new permissions, network calls, credential handling, or approval bypass. The duplicate-session check runs after the existing exec target/security/workdir/approval path. Execution identity is stored as an opaque SHA-256 hash, not raw environment data.

## Human Verification

- Reproduced the pre-fix runtime behavior with two direct `runExecProcess` calls: `before_fix_running_sessions=2`.
- Ran the after-fix runtime proof above and reviewed the exec/process path.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Checked `.github/CODEOWNERS`; the touched `src/agents/bash-*` files do not match the restricted auth/sandbox/secops entries. Expected review is normal agent runtime maintainer review.

## Changelog

No contributor `CHANGELOG.md` edit.

## Review conversations

- Target issue search found no linked or exact open PR for `62432`.
- Related closed/stale PRs were checked for fix shape and maintainer intent.
- ClawSweeper and local `codex review --base origin/main` found three concrete issues during drafting: option-drift reuse, pre-spawn phantom sessions, and foreground rerun collapse. This patch addresses all three with tests.
- The final local autoreview rerun after the foreground-rerun adjustment was blocked by a Codex usage-limit error before producing a verdict.

## Risks

An explicit background/yield request that intentionally wants a second identical concurrent process in the same scoped process identity needs a distinct execution option or command text. Plain foreground reruns are preserved.

## Behavior tradeoffs

The guard uses exact execution identity instead of fuzzy command similarity. That keeps the fix narrow and avoids guessing intent from generated text.

## Scope boundaries

- No provider-specific Xiaomi/MiMo routing change.
- No loop-detection threshold or config change.
- No process persistence, schema, docs, or public config change.
- Completed-session reruns and plain foreground reruns remain allowed.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`
- Real runtime proof above
target.author: ferminquant

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
